item.title: Victims ID'd in B-52 bomber crash that killed 8 at Edwards Air Force Base
item.description: As standard practice, the military makes public identifications 24 hours after next of kin have been notified.

item.title: Jesse Welles on "This Land is Your Land" and Woody Guthrie's legacy
item.description: In the series "USA to Z," which celebrates 250 years of American history and culture, Anthony Mason talks to singer-songwriter Jesse Welles about Woody Guthrie's legacy and the meaning behind the popular song "This Land is Your Land."

item.title: Dow falls after Fed holds interest rates steady
item.description: The Federal Reserve maintained its benchmark interest rate on Wednesday in its first meeting with Kevin Warsh as chair. CBS News MoneyWatch correspondent Kelly O'Grady reports from the New York Stock Exchange.

item.title: Trump says U.S. would "do better as a country" without USMCA
item.description: Speaking to reporters in Paris, France, on Wednesday, President Trump criticized the United States-Mexico-Canada Agreement, saying he would "rather not have" it. Mr. Trump signed the trade deal during his first term.

item.title: Trump says Iran deal not final, will resume bombing "if they don't behave"
item.description: President Trump warns he could order new strikes if Iran's leaders "don't behave," two days before the expected signing of a deal that remains vaguely defined.

item.title: Pastors for Trump founder drops out of House race after texting scandal
item.description: Jackson Lahmeyer had been backed by President Trump, although the president dropped his endorsement shortly before Lahmeyer dropped out.

item.title: Dramatic rescue efforts after fiery small plane crash in Texas kills 1
item.description: A business jet with six on board crashed on a Laredo, Texas, highway and caught fire, killing one person and causing chaos as passersby frantically tried to save those inside.

item.title: Federal Reserve holds interest rates steady but leaves door open to hike
item.description: U.S. inflation is expected to remain elevated through the end of the year, Fed officials say in their latest forecast.

item.title: Senate postpones Clayton's confirmation hearing after Trump upends plans
item.description: The Senate canceled Jay Clayton's confirmation hearing on Wednesday after President Trump's move to delay the installation of the new intelligence chief.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $35,000 money market account can earn now
item.description: A $35,000 money market account can result in a big return on your money, but it's not the only advantage to know now.

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers will argue psychiatric defense in NYS murder trial
item.description: Luigi Mangione's lawyers will argue that he was suffering from an extreme emotional disturbance when he allegedly killed UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson.

item.title: CBS to host "The Great American Block Party 250" special on July 4
item.description: CBS will host a primetime special celebrating America's 250th birthday on Saturday, July 4, with exclusive performances and the largest fireworks show in history.

item.title: CBS to host "The Great American Block Party 250" on July 4
item.description: "CBS Evening News" anchor Tony Dokoupil and "Entertainment Tonight" co-host Nischelle Turner will host "The Great American Block Party 250," a primetime special on July 4 celebrating America's 250th birthday from Washington, D.C.

item.title: Read the 14 points of the agreement between Iran and the U.S.
item.description: The agreement, as read by senior U.S. officials, allows Iran to immediately begin exporting oil and petroleum products.

item.title: Rex Heuermann sentenced to life in prison for Gilgo Beach serial killings
item.description: Rex Heuermann was sentenced to life in prison without the possibility of parole for the series of murders known as the Gilgo Beach serial killings.

item.title: Trump on why Iran deal doesn't include more on nuclear weapons
item.description: President Trump told CBS News' Nancy Cordes that he would bomb "the hell" out of Iran if they don't adhere to the latest agreement, despite the text not including much about developing nuclear weapons.

item.title: Trump touts Iran agreement to wrap up G7 summit in France
item.description: President Trump defended the agreement reached by the U.S. and Iran​ at a press conference to close out the G7 summit in France.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Suspects in alleged UFC attack plot at White House wanted to use drones, explosives and snipers
item.description: More details are emerging on the suspects in the alleged plot to attack the UFC event at the White House on Sunday. 19 people were involved in the alleged scheme, the FBI said. CBS News' Sarah Lynch has the latest.

item.title: Jay Clayton's DNI confirmation hearing postponed
item.description: The Senate Intelligence Committee on Wednesday postponed Jay Clayton's confirmation hearing for director of national intelligence after President Trump said in a social media post he wouldn't appear. In the announcement, Mr. Trump made multiple demands to Congress. CBS News' Taurean Small has more.

item.title: Here's why a gold investment makes sense right now
item.description: The benefits of a gold investment this June are multiple. Here's why it's especially worth investing in right now.

item.title: Tropical Storm Arthur forms off Texas Gulf Coast
item.description: Tropical Storm Arthur, the first named storm of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season, formed Wednesday off the Gulf Coast of Texas.

item.title: Suspect in custody after deadly shooting at Wilmington Hospital
item.description: A suspect was taken into custody in Philadelphia in connection with the shooting at Wilmington Hospital that left a 19-year-old dead and another injured.

item.title: Fed votes to leave interest rates unchanged in first decision with Kevin Warsh as chair
item.description: The Federal Reserve board voted Wednesday to leave interest rates unchanged in its first decision since Kevin Warsh took over as chair. CBS News' Kelly O'Grady has more.

item.title: Trump meets with India's Modi at G7 as he navigates global interest in Iran deal
item.description: President Trump met with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi at the G7 summit, and emphasized that the two had a "very good" conversation despite past tensions between the two nations. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe has more context.

item.title: What the Fed rate pause could mean for mortgage interest rates now
item.description: The Fed held rates steady again on Wednesday. Here's what that continued pause could mean for mortgage borrowers.

item.title: The Obama Presidential Center and Library opens in Chicago
item.description: After more than a decade since it was announced, the Obama Presidential Center and Library officially opens to the public in Chicago's Jackson Park on June 19, 2026.

item.title: Trump invokes Defense Production Act to boost weapons manufacturing
item.description: President Trump has invoked the Defense Production Act to address constraints in the production of munitions, according to a presidential memo released Tuesday.

item.title: Tom Homan, Trump's border czar, reacts to abuse allegations at Delaney Hall ICE facility
item.description: Tom Homan, President Trump's border czar, is reacting to prolonged protests outside New Jersey's Delaney Hall ICE facility amid allegations of inhumane conditions for detained migrants. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: About Us
item.description: "60 Minutes" airs Sundays at 7 p.m. ET/PT
